title: RIO Protocol Nonce Registry Entry
description: A schema for a single entry in the RIO Nonce Registry. This registry is a critical component for preventing replay attacks within the RIO Protocol by ensuring that each nonce is consumed exactly once. An entry is created when a nonce is used in a transaction, and it is retained until its specified expiry time.

nonce.description: The unique, single-use cryptographic nonce value. This value MUST be generated by the client and is used to prevent replay attacks. The nonce SHALL be of sufficient length and randomness to make collisions computationally infeasible.

authorization_id.description: The UUID of the authorization record that utilized this nonce. This creates a verifiable link between the nonce and the specific authorization it was consumed under, which is essential for audit and traceability.

request_id.description: The UUID of the original canonical request associated with this nonce. This allows for tracing the nonce back to the initial action request that entered the RIO Protocol.

consumed_at.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp indicating the exact moment the nonce was consumed. This timestamp is critical for establishing the temporal sequence of events and for identifying potential timing-based attacks.

consumed_by.description: An object identifying the entity that consumed the nonce. This provides clarity on which component of the RIO ecosystem (e.g., an executor or a validation service) was responsible for its use.

executor_id.description: The unique identifier of the executor or service that consumed the nonce.

execution_id.description: The UUID of the execution record that consumed the nonce. If the nonce was consumed during a pre-execution validation step rather than by a final executor, this value MAY be null. This links the nonce directly to the final action taken.

status.description: The current status of the nonce. 'consumed' indicates it has been successfully used. 'invalidated' means it was voided before use. 'expired' means the time window for its use has passed. The status MUST be one of these values.

expiry.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp defining when this nonce registry entry is no longer considered active and MAY be purged from the registry. This is a resource management feature and SHOULD be set to a duration sufficient for audit and dispute resolution purposes.

notes.description: An optional field for any human-readable notes or contextual information related to the nonce consumption. This field MAY be used for debugging or audit clarification.
